programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A pedestrian is rushed to AandE with multiple life-threatening injuries after being injured in a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours in...
programme.desc: Jessica Fostekew and Joe enjoy a Canarian caper in Lanzarote, as they trike through the lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and explore the Jameos del Agua lava tunnels.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A couple create an ambitious floating home, two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home, and George and Will conjure up a magical 30-mile vista in a wall just 20cm thick, in their secret-garden studio flat.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Lambing season continues, and the family build a field shelter to keep an eye on the ewes. And Matt and Mike add some finishing touches to the stable yard.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Bulgaria, dad and son Colin and Paul turn a barn into an entertainment space. In Spain, Jo and Ken tackle a lull in business, and Becky and Kay prep for their first guests.

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: No stranger to helping others find their perfect place in the sun, Jasmine Harman takes the plunge and transforms a run-down villa into a family home on the Costa del Sol.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Rod and Margaret are selling up in London and want to buy a house in Dorset, near the seaside town of Weymouth, as well as a holiday home in the Dordogne, in the south west of France.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman lines up five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget, but are any perfect for them?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness instructor Tanya Manock hosts a stylish Mediterranean feast for her guests at the start of a week of competitive dining in Rochdale. However, is this glamourpuss host more style than substance?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night two in Rochdale and business coach and passionate foodie Rod Tonge is determined to impress. However, he has already offended glamorous fitness instructor Tanya and HR manager Claire Lemon predicts fireworks at the table.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night three in Rochdale and HR manager Claire Lemon hopes to compensate for `imperfect cooking skills with her bubbly personality and superior hosting ability. However, tension rises when Tanya barely touches the starter or the main.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Psychic medium Dean Fox plans to put his supernatural skills to good use on night four in Rochdale. The main goes down very well, even with Tanya, only for the pudding to bomb.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the last night in Rochdale and the turn of feisty mum Sadiya Hussein to host. Will the tension between Sadiya and Tanya finally explode?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on one of their most turbulent builds ever: Amber and Martins working from home transformation in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George attempts to bring the grandeur back to a Georgian town house in Cheshire thats been stripped of its original features, and tackles a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start. But Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

programme.title: Britains Most Luxurious Train Journeys
programme.desc: Step inside a world of fine dining and opulent carriages for a trip to Kingswear on the Devonshire coast. The steam-hauled train, with its plush interiors, decadent food service and magnificent vistas, is as impressive as the destination itself.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aleshe make decorated traybakes, tarte tatin and recreate their childhood toys in biscuits.

programme.title: Sarah Millican: Control Enthusiast
programme.desc: Sarah Millican returns to the stage with her show covering topics such as road rage, IBS, her favourite word and how she lets her husband know that `tonights the night.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A musical waiter, a Boris-loving singleton, and an 80s-obsessed romantic - this time First Dates serves up chaos over a spilled drink, a bit of chemistry and a few curveballs.

programme.title: Benedetta
programme.desc: A 17th-century nun becomes entangled in a forbidden lesbian affair, but its her shocking religious visions that threaten to shake the Church to its very core.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie are missing one thing from their family: a woman to turn them into a throuple. And glamour puss Chrissie is looking for husband number four.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Married parents Tom and Kirsty check in to fulfil Toms fantasy of experimenting with another man. Natalie and Tom explore a threesome with Precious, insecurities could get in the way.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Second up in Northamptonshire is funeral service manager Peter Pickering, who is dead set on hosting a traditional dinner party reminiscent of the 1920s. However, his guests dont like the sound of his bread and butter pudding.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Stay-at-home mum Stephanie Reeders nerves are evident from the outset on her night in Northamptonshire. Shes planning to serve classic fish and chips while offering a glamorous swishing session for fun.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tweny-six-year-old business planning manager Lauren Oxlade is the penultimate host in Northamptonshire. She is an organised and passionate cook and has a few surprising friends who shes invited along to her party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Last to try his hand in Northamptonshire is managing director and proud northerner Carl Johnson, with a menu that includes gammon cooked in two litres of cola.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at some of their most resplendent renovations, and deliver handy tips and tricks to help us maximise the spend on our precious bricks and mortar.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out whats next on their demolition list.

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: In Nottingham, Emma and Matts hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. Its a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Fred Dibnah continues his tour of Britains great building feats with a look at our places of work. In Chatham he visits one of our oldest dockyards, while at Armley Mills in Leeds he discovers an early form of fireproofing.

programme.title: Spring Walks
programme.desc: Spring is in the air as Sara explores the lush Swinton Estate in North Yorkshire, A keen walker, Sara often rambles with family and friends near her Teesside home. She is accompanied only by a 360-degree camera, breathtaking landscapes and her thoughts. Sara opens up about the challenges she faced as a young businesswoman. She reveals the unexpected impact of participating in `Strictly Come Dancing and reflects on the importance of family support.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth has her feathers ruffled when she has problems with Daddy and is appalled to spy a strange man at Elizabeths house who has obviously spent the night there. What will it do to property values?

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.sub-title: Does You Mother Know Youre Out
programme.desc: Incompetence and an interfering parent conspire to wreck Tims plans to see `Gone With The Wind.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama looks at one of the greatest horrors of medieval Britain - the Black Death. Those who didnt perish went on to suffer decades of anarchy and unrest, but who would have thought the country gent would emerge from the turmoil as a breed of survivor?

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary. This intimate and pioneering film explores a three-way relationship and a radically different approach to fatherhood.

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: The birdsong of sunrise in all its uninterrupted glory, free from the voice over and music of traditional television.

programme.title: Pompeii: The Mystery of the People Frozen in Time
programme.desc: The city of Pompeii uniquely captures the publics imagination; in 79AD a legendary volcanic disaster left its citizens preserved in ashes to this very day. Yet no-one has been able to unravel the full story that is at the heart of our fascination: how did those bodies become frozen in time? For the first time the BBC has been granted unique access to these strange, ghost-like body casts.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cute critters and caring keepers. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle explore Longleat Safari Park, meeting a host of exotic, fascinating animals and the people who look after them.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Firefighters and farmers tackle Bedfordshire field fires, rescue crews respond to a horse in danger, and Paul Colgan helps volunteers protect wildlife from illegal badger baiters.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The search for a vulnerable young woman unravels a tragic case of neglect and murder. And police investigating the murder of a popular teen find the key suspects very close to home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Scarlett Moffatt
programme.desc: Turn the Gogglebox on and tune in to see Martin and special celebrity guest presenter Scarlett Moffatt take a tour of a Sunderland property that has them looking to break out the champagne. We also delve into the archives from 2007 to see a London studio flat thats at a price Martin just cant believe. And X marks the spot for some dodgy patio doors for Jacqui in Surrey.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp has a puzzle for passers-by in Brackley to solve. Her mystery object is heavy and made of cast iron with a brass opening. What could it be and why is it so important for one avid collector. The venue for the shopping showdown is an antiques centre in the Northamptonshire town. Engaged couple Aron and Charlie are bargain-hunting with Thomas Forrester, while married couple Lucille and Geoff are with Tim Weeks.

programme.desc: Roman Kemp suggests a reverse-moves approach to exercise, including walking downhill instead of up and lowering weights instead of lifting them, both of which could have significant health benefits.

programme.sub-title: Can Churchill Get Ann to Africa?
programme.desc: A former pig farmer makes a last-ditch attempt to clear her home of unwanted belongings and antiques before old age prevents her decade-long fundraising campaign for an African charity.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Sussex to help a couple from a Berkshire commuter town escape to the country. They have requested a unique home, and the quirkier, the better! The selection on offer includes some truly wow-factor properties, any one of which could be the mystery house. Denise also visits the Royal Botanic Gardens at Wakehurst to discover how climate change could shape the way we all garden in the future.

programme.desc: Will and Dom learn how a devastating fire put a 5,000-year-old craft in jeopardy, Chris Shaw repairs a Gaelic pipe music book, and Dom has the chance to make his own sporran.

programme.desc: In Surrey, Alison wants to downsize a lifetime of clutter that has filled the family home and garage. She hopes that by selling heirlooms and treasures she will be able to take her two grown-up children on a holiday of a lifetime.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: House-hunting in the gorgeous Devon countryside, Jules Hudson helps two retired drama teachers trade busy Sheffield for some coastal calm. Jules has some splendid properties up his sleeve, including a lovely period cottage boasting a magnificent garden just a stones throw from the beach. Along the way, he also explores a 550-acre rewilding project brimming with wildflower meadows and teeming with wildlife.

programme.desc: The team face their quirkiest fix yet - a giant fibreglass cow lantern. Also, luthier Julyan brings a banjulele back to life, and Dom gets a crash course in brilliant cutting.

programme.title: 21 Bridges
programme.desc: After uncovering a massive conspiracy, an embattled NYPD detective joins a citywide manhunt for two young cop killers. As the night unfolds, he soon becomes unsure of who to pursue -- and whos in pursuit of him. programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Firefighters and farmers tackle Bedfordshire field fires, rescue crews respond to a horse in danger, and Paul Colgan helps volunteers protect wildlife from illegal badger baiters.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: The search for a vulnerable young woman unravels a tragic case of neglect and murder. And police investigating the murder of a popular teen find the key suspects very close to home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Martin and Scarlett Moffatt
programme.desc: Turn the Gogglebox on and tune in to see Martin and special celebrity guest presenter Scarlett Moffatt take a tour of a Sunderland property that has them looking to break out the champagne. We also delve into the archives from 2007 to see a London studio flat thats at a price Martin just cant believe. And X marks the spot for some dodgy patio doors for Jacqui in Surrey.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp has a puzzle for passers-by in Brackley to solve. Her mystery object is heavy and made of cast iron with a brass opening. What could it be and why is it so important for one avid collector. The venue for the shopping showdown is an antiques centre in the Northamptonshire town. Engaged couple Aron and Charlie are bargain-hunting with Thomas Forrester, while married couple Lucille and Geoff are with Tim Weeks.

programme.desc: A former pig farmer makes a last-ditch attempt to clear her home of unwanted belongings and antiques before old age prevents her decade-long fundraising campaign for an African charity.

programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Sussex to help a couple from a Berkshire commuter town escape to the country. They have requested a unique home, and the quirkier, the better! programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Clive takes on the star goal scorer for ACF Fiorentinas womens team in a penalty shoot-out. Back in the city, he indulges his passion for fashion - his mum Lynne was a seamstress for Burberry, and his first job was on Black Beauty and Hair magazine - by getting a masterclass in Italian menswear. Clive also discovers why italics are so Italian as he designs a wedding card for his niece Lucy.

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: George gets up close and personal with Siswi, an orang-utan, heads 100 metres underground to a secluded monkey dormitory, and investigates baboons with a thirst for flamingo flesh.

programme.desc: Items in Salford include an album of Victorian photographs, Zulu wedding beads, an American scrimshaw whales tooth and a drawing by local artist L S Lowry.

programme.desc: Each week a group of four celebrities compete to test their knowledge in a series of quick-paced rounds of games. Host, Richard Osman then cross-examines the celebrities as they strive to increase their scores and match Richards mental prowess.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Lincolnshire is a county of farming innovation, where Rick meets inventor James Dyson, who allows him special access to his farm of the future. Here, strawberries, tended by robots, are grown all year round.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Lochs
programme.desc: Paul sails along a much-loved route through the Kyles of Bute to Dunoon, discovering a secret history of WW2 bouncing bombs along the way. From Dunoon and its connections with international singing star Harry Lauder, Paul gets nostalgic about nuclear submarines in the Holy Loch.

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.title: Whats New Scooby-Doo?
programme.desc: The Secret Six puppies are dog-napped from the dog show, and its up to Scooby and the gang to rescue them.

programme.title: Odd Squad

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven eats a bad meatball.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series

programme.desc: Liko and Roy reunite with one of their friends from the Rising Volt Tacklers.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings find a magic stick that functions as a Yummy divining rod, but Grizzy steals their stick and finds a Yummy deposit in the savannah. To take control over Grizzys discovery, the Lemmings set a trap to draw the bear.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: Its opening night for the sold-out senior show! After weeks of rehearsals and complicated set builds, tickets are sold out - but will the set hold up and the cast be ready in time?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Take My Breath Away! Dr Chris, Dr Xand and Dr Ronx explore their way through the world of medicine and biology.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.sub-title: Even Better Than the Real Thing
programme.desc: The team compete in the first round at regionals. Due to a misreading of the rules, the solo round is not what they expected, and the result is devastating.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife and the Vipers are back for more basketball, more rewinds and even more chaos!

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel. Programmes start at 6.00am.

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: Follows the adventures of a young bear called Tish, her bear family and her larger-than-life imaginary friend Tash, as they learn about the world around them.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing discovers a hole in his favourite yellow wellies, but he doesnt want to throw them away. With Flops help, Bing finds a way to recycle the wellies into colourful flower pots.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa follow a trail of muddy footprints and find Po in bed with very muddy feet. The Noo-noo cleans off the mud.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wants to watch his favourite TV show, `Bubble Duck, but Mo is already watching hers. The battle over the TV remote, to see who gains control of the television, soon gets out of hand.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Follow the adventures of Supertato, the worlds first potato superhero as he tackles the mischievous Evil Pea, in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are building a stone tower in the Howly Woods when Sula finds an ammonite. Bing wants to find one too but is overwhelmed by the amount of stones, which makes it very difficult to find one. A frustrated Bing sits down, and Sula notices some ammonites in the rock behind him. Flop takes a picture of Bing and Sula with their ammonites.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo fix Ted up as a pirate ship for the Pirate Parade, but swashbuckling Capn Dot hijacks him. Team Mojo must out-pirate Dot and sail the streets to stop her.

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Today at Squirrel Club, there are all sorts of comparisons to learn, from small to smallest, hungry to hungriest, and Duggee to Duggiest!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly has lost his best potato, but Duggee finds it using his sense of smell. The Squirrels learn about the five senses and earn their Senses Badge.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo build a very special cubby for their stuffed toy Kimjim.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo spend the day at Nanas and want everyone to play charades, but their cousins Muffin and Socks are too young to understand the rules. Nana must quickly think of how to keep the game on track.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.sub-title: Chinese New Year, Dragon Dance Drama
programme.desc: Kyans invited to perform a dragon dance for Chinese New Year in Beijing. Glitch decides to build a fire breathing dragon robot for the parade and The Go Jetters need to stop it!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Mums Precious Things
programme.desc: When Peter accidentally discards his mothers most precious possessions he must retrieve them from Tommy Brock!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five arrives to get the band together, and the party started, with a big high five.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: U gets the balloon ride of his life, but hes still unhappy - or so he thinks! When unhappy U meets perky P, things can only go one way - up. Now Us only problem is getting back down.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny are excited about Bad Idea Puppys birthday party. First, however, they need to find him the perfect epic presents from the Everything But Sprinkles Store. They head to the Epic Ideas Department.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays with her favourite cup - it makes the perfect drum for a sing along with Lift. Together, they visit Kingsley and Queenie who play the Tea Party Dash, but who will win?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its the magical wish granter Mango, ready to make all the cast wishes come true.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Bing are camping in Bings garden in order to see the stars and the moon. As Flop goes back for Hoppity, night-time noises seep from the dark garden, spooking Bing. Then, a scary shape on the tent terrifies him. They go inside, but the monster has followed them in! Bing and Sula discover that Flop is the monster. So they go back outside, where the billion, zillion stars and a huge moon are waiting for them.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `robot, and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. See a red robot, a smiling robot, a building robot, a sleeping robot and a helping robot. Dee meets a bleeping robot and has a boogie with a group of dancing robots. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dees friend finally succeeds in saying todays word.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu has trouble waiting for her turn to play with a toy.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos visit an hotel and find themselves hiding behind a line of suitcases. One by one, the porter takes each suitcase away, and the Twirlywoos move down the line, hiding behind different cases until they have nowhere else to hide.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin and his friends go on a treasure hunt. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble has baked a cake for Aunt Pollys birthday, but the cake ends up having an adventure all of its own.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubby Custard Machine is broken, but The Noo-noo fixes it. The Teletubbies go on a Tubby Custard Ride and make a lovely custardy mess inside and outside Home Dome.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Luna, Arlo, Oakley, Lily-Rose, Reece and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. All the parents and babies have different mealtime-themed objects and toys which are explored through play, songs and a story.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee meets her new friend Jacob in the city and plays a game of I Spy.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: It is a busy day down in the garden, as Aaron, Adrian and Nelly are looking for some minibeasts! Their journey starts when they lift some logs and stones to see what is underneath, uncovering a family of woodlice scuttling around.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is trying to win a lasso contest, but first, theres some making to be done with Murphi. A toothpaste box and a toothpaste tube are transformed into a toothpaste tube snake, the Mini Monsters create something beastly out of burger boxes, and Rebeccas cousin calls on the Magic Monster Mirror. Finally, a large cardboard tube provides inspiration for a terrific tube vulture.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas meet the stinky Onion-Rexes who help stop the nasty Bananaraptors.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mum and Dad construct their own magic claw machine at home for Bluey and Bingo to play with, but Dad makes sure winning isnt easy.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Its bridge building time. The Squirrels help a quarrelling group of Gerbils sort out their longstanding disagreement.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Five Speckled Frogs
programme.desc: We sing a calming song about counting frogs with Mrs Weaver.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.sub-title: Magical Door and Stompy Bridge Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to the plant nursery to plant some seeds. On the way they spot shapes on a pebbly path, go through a magical door, cross a stompy bridge and even dance like elves!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Henry and his little sister Sophie make crowns and become King Henry and Queen Sophie. Along with their noble knights, Mummy and Mama, they head to the local park on a royal quest to rescue their toy turtle Pebbles, who is trapped in a castle.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Jenae is cooking her favourite meal of Jamaican saltfish and cabbage. Her family are Jamaican, and in Jamaica saltfish and cabbage is a very popular meal. Lots of people eat it for their breakfast, but Jenae is cooking it as a very special dinner for her friends. She is using saltfish, fresh cabbage and colourful peppers in her meal. Jenaes friends have mixed opinions about the taste of fresh green pepper. She hopes that they will like it cooked in her recipe.

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Ishi wants to find out all about barnacles, so Chantelle meets some new friends at the beach and they head off to look for barnacles, whilst Rory meets a marine expert and they get their feet wet.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Diesel is a hearing dog for his teenage owner, Rayane, who is deaf. This means he doesnt hear everything. Diesel is trained to listen out for important sounds that Rayane might miss. Diesel is Rayanes ears. Together, theyre unstoppable! Rayane needs Diesels help to do his Sunday chores. But first, Rayane teaches Diesel a new command - find it. When Rayane squeaks his squeaker and makes the sign, Diesel has to find it. Diesel loves learning new commands!

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some Asian elephants and give them a shower with a giant hose. They see how the elephants use their trunk in lots of different ways and discover more about these very clever animals. Their next animal is the ring-tailed lemur, which they meet face to face and learn what they use their long stripey tails for. Finally, Steve and Aneeshwar find what the wild connection is between these two animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evies mum, Jess, has just married Dougs dad, Simon. The new family get home to discover a lost puppy hiding in their wedding presents. Evie wants to keep him and call him Waffle.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Animated series for children.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this reggae song, the Small Potatoes sing about how wonderful it is to be alive and feel the `Potato Love.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: TapTaps loud, fast drumming is driving everyone in the garden to distraction. Can Yukee and Twinkle find a way to show TapTap that fast doesnt necessarily have to mean loud?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has made a lovely pie for everyone to share, but slicing a pie five ways is tricky. The Squirrels try to figure out the best to share equally.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `moon and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We learn all about the moon.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Fives new smoothie cafe is open! Can the Numberblocks get the orders right?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: O has the most extraordinary day ever, with a goat on a boat, a mole in a hole and a magic golden discovery.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a fairytale castle for a musical adventure, while Fairy Godmother Tumble has a fairytale adventure of his own.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies cant get enough of the Tubby Custard Ride so they ask to go on it again and again! In Tummy Tales, children play on a slide again and again.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Hummingbird has a problem: he wants to drink sweet nectar but the flowers are closed! Will the Patchwork Pals find a way to help him?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: All the animals are having a nap, but Raa Raa and Ooo Ooo arent tired and want to play. Will they have enough energy to last until everyone else wakes up?

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Hoot and Toot have had enough. The twins bickering has finally led them to separate and go different ways. But now theyre lost without each other.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Gail Force reports live from a new playground and is very keen to try out all the new equipment. Gardener Will Barrow waters some very funny flowers and Professor Muddles has got a brand new invention to help tidy away toys.

programme.title: Andys Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads back in time to search a prehistoric forest for a plant-munching dinosaur, but he runs into a relative of the T-rex instead, before joining a herd of centrosaurus to escape a flash flood.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Catchers land on planet Cartoon and make friends with two cute alien kids: Toby and Penny. Their favourite game is Shoot the Can.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.sub-title: Octonauts and the Poison Dart Frogs
programme.desc: Whether its a perilous voyage to the bottom of the sea or the piratey pursuit of a giant squid, the Octonauts leap into action. The Octonauts find out about poison dart frogs.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Jeremy Fishers Recital
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Mr. Tod crash Jeremy Fishers annual lake-side get together, its up to Peter to save the party.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has made a time machine to show the Squirrels famous periods in time.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Its Healthy Fur Day, and almost all the animals in Sweetwood are coming to Vidas clinic to get groomed. However, Zigzag, a bunny with some serious knots in her fluffy tail, is an exception.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea tells the story of when she and Supertato first met as young veggies.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings find a device that can make Weasy do what they want.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Fabios yummy new sauce is so sticky that he gets stuck to Mojos bonnet. They have to do everything together until Bo figures out how to unsticky that sauce!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo build a very special cubby for their stuffed toy Kimjim.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris are delighted when the berg hits a tropical island and move into holiday mode. But when their wasteful behaviour annoys the local monkeys, they need to make amends.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo spend the day at Nanas and want everyone to play charades, but their cousins Muffin and Socks are too young to understand the rules. Nana must quickly think of how to keep the game on track.

programme.title: Andys Dino Island
programme.sub-title: Andy and the Dino Tree Mystery
programme.desc: Jen is doing a study of all the plants on Dino Island and has asked Andy to take photos of some of the rare plant specimens. While photographing them, Andy discovers unusual markings on some of the trees. With Jens help, he sets out to discover whats making them and reveals an unlikely dinosaur culprit. Along the way, he meets a herd of Pachycephalosaurus and watches two males headbutting each other in a show of dominance.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: Clangers

programme.title: Beddybyes

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Q, Murr and Sal finally clean out their garages before giving truly spooky presentations; in the end, David Cross brings a sense of danger to the losing joker and makes a memory theyre sure to remember.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray discovers that Debra becomes more passionate when she argues with Marie, so he concocts a series of lies in an attempt to boost his love life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray has to tell Debra that the PTA has a problem with the way she dresses.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray are shocked when Ally comes home with an `F on her report card.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The women pressure Ray and Frank to accompany Robert to one of his therapy sessions. Instead, they head to the racetrack.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Dermot OLeary, Dame Jessica Ennis-Hill, Roman Kemp, Adam Kay, Angela Scanlon, Kay Burley and Alex Brooker try to help the contestants answer their way to a fortune.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey stumbles upon a way to impress a famous actor during an audition, Ross learns the identity of a long-ago mugger, and Chandler accepts an internship at a shoe company.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Boob Job
programme.desc: Monica secretly asks Joey for a loan because she and Chandler are strapped for cash, Phoebe invites her boyfriend to move in with her, and Rachels apartment baby-proofing drives Joey crazy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Memorial Service
programme.desc: When Chandler jokingly posts an outrageous biography about Ross on Ross college alumni website, Ross retaliates in kind, sparking an internet war. Meanwhile, Monica gets extremely vigilant after a weak-willed Phoebe asks for help to stop her from contacting Mike after their relationship ends.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Hoping to win a huge jackpot, the friends pool their money and buy dozens of lottery tickets, but they argue over how to spend the fortune if they win, and Monica irritates everyone else by buying tickets just for her and Chandler. Exasperated, Phoebe takes the bowl of everyones tickets and threatens to throw it off the balcony. The others make up, but a pigeon startles Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: A visit to the set of a soap opera inspires Rachel to dream of a romantic encounter with Joey, and Chandler must take Ross on a weekend getaway after Monica is unable to take time off from work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party
programme.desc: Ross is excited when he meets a stunning palaeontology professor, but when he takes her to Joeys rooftop party for the actors soap-opera co-stars, she intimidates him with her impressive list of former boyfriends. Meanwhile, Rachel and an intoxicated Monica make fools of themselves at the party chasing after the hunky daytime actors.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fertility Test
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler have an embarrassing encounter with an old acquaintance at a fertility clinic, Ross plays Cyrano to help Joey woo a professor, and Rachel sneaks into a spa for a massage hoping Phoebe wont find out.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. When Chandler invites a handsome colleague round for dinner, Monica and Chandler embarrassingly interview him as a potential sperm donor. Meanwhile, while shopping with Phoebe, Rachel admits that she still has a thing for Joey.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang heads to Barbados for a palaeontology convention where Ross is the keynote speaker. Rachel harbours steamy thoughts about Joey while David prepares to propose to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Joey and Rachel Kiss
programme.desc: While the friends are still in Barbados, Joey tries to tell Ross about his feelings for Rachel and Ross wants to confess to kissing Joeys girlfriend Charlie, but it is easier said than done. Meanwhile, Monica, Chandler and Phoebe can hear their amorous conversations through the hotels paper-thin walls. Also, Monicas hair problems go from bad to worse, and Phoebe has some bad news for Mikes girlfriend on her birthday.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Is Fine
programme.desc: Ross drinks too much wine while dining with Rachel, Joey and Charlie. Elsewhere, Frank Jr cannot cope with his precocious triplets and pays Phoebe a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Joey nervously anticipate their first real date, Monica and Phoebe try to avoid an obnoxious friend with a fake British accent, and Ross emerges from a tanning booth with a two-toned appearance.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gets Monica and Chandler to delay their long-awaited trip to Vermont for Emmas first birthday bash, but problems ensue when Emma falls asleep and a racy cake turns up.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachels Sister Baby-Sits
programme.desc: Rachel regrets trusting her self-indulgent little sister Amy with Emma, Monica regrets asking Joey to write a recommendation letter to the child adoption agency for her, and Phoebe accidentally thwarts the anniversary surprise Mike has in store for her.

programme.title: True Lies
programme.desc: Explosive tale about a US undercover agent who draws his frustrated wife into his dangerous world in a bid to give her the excitement she craves. For her, the experience of battling fanatical terrorists is totally unexpected, as she believes she is married to a computer salesman and not an action man.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Hollywood director Colin rejigs a casino robbery scene using demonic possession and `Les Miserables. Olympian Misty-May Treanor brings the muscle in a Worlds Strongest Women Competition.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway? USA
programme.desc: Lonely heart Heather must choose between Waynes Chewbacca, Colins submarine skipper and Ryans evil disembodied head. Ryan and Colin insert random snatches of dialogue into a `Titanic scene.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Randy is rallying his troops, the zombie masses are restless outside the mall, and King Cartman prepares for battle with Princess Kenny - Black Friday is upon us!

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: While Wendy tries to expose the Photoshop fakery of pictures of women, Kanye West tries to prove to the world his fiancee isnt a hobbit.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The off-colour adventures of four elementary school boys.

programme.desc: The side-splitting grand finale of Lee Evans 100 date sell-out UK tour at the Edinburgh Playhouse.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: With a family facing ruin, setbacks, and soaring debt on their BC mine, its up to Juan and prospecting experts Jeff and Sophia Bond to save them. Using 6,000 pounds of steel, Juan creates his largest-ever hopper build to get a massive gold increase.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: With a family facing ruin, setbacks, and soaring debt on their BC mine, its up to Juan and prospecting experts Jeff and Sophia Bond to save them. Using 6,000 pounds of steel, Juan creates his largest-ever hopper build to get a massive gold increase.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot

But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Dave Kims parents out of town, Adam throws a party to impress Brea. Beverly lets fame go to her head after her cookbook is published.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Philadelphia to visit a Mexican restaurant called Zocalo. Greg and Mary Russell loved the place so much that they pooled their resources and bought it. However, it seems that Cupids arrow had a poisoned tip: the restaurant has become an albatross around their necks.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit, leaving the experiment permanently shaken.

programme.title: Peep Show

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark is still having doubts about Sophie so he decides to join a gym, and Jeremy decides to get a job there after Mark spots his ex-wife Nancy.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.sub-title: Couples: Portsmouth and The Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Maddi and her husband Givon start the competition with a menu inspired by their Sri Lankan heritage. Second to host are Kate and Dan who prepare a night of rustic Italian cooking, and finally its time for Ali and John to step up to the plate.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first hosts are pensioners Bill and Hilary, with an evening of classic dishes, day two sees Doug and Patsy host with a combination of fine dining and hippie happenings, and finally its time for youngsters Micaela and Sanchez to impress.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Rachel and Anthonys home looks great at first glance, but its awkward layout and limited storage are cramping their style.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back Liam plans to wow his bride with a wintry white wedding. But 2000 miles away in Portugal, bride-to-be Georgia is dreaming of sun, sand and sangria.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Thai Stag Do
programme.desc: In a bid to save money to put on a show-stopping wedding for Jay, his down-to-earth bride, Jack-the-lad Ian flies himself and his two best men 12,000 miles away to Thailand.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The CEO of Buffalo Wings and Rings meets a kitchen manager who rules his kitchen with an iron fist and discovers that one of his franchisees isnt respecting his employees.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.desc: Shawnon Bellah fails to meet her own customer service requirements when she is undercover.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Sammy, the owner of Sabatiellos restaurant in Stamford, Conn., is more than $1 million in debt when he asks for chef Gordon Ramsays help; when Gordon criticizes the food, Sammys temper flares out of control.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits Trobianos of Great Neck, N.Y., an Italian restaurant that is a half-million dollars in debt; during a confrontation with the owner about the cleanliness of the kitchen and the quality of the food, Ramsay walks out.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Fireman Dan and nurse Olivia are desperate for a fresh start, but their current fixer-upper is holding them back.

programme.title: Below Deck
programme.desc: Fraser must deal with escalating tension between two of his stews that threatens to ruin his season; Jared makes a big mistake, causing Ben and Fraser to feel that they cant rely on the new bosun; Capt. Kerry docks St. David for the first time.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: With the final in sight, there is everything to play for and Mike Wozniak issues a stark warning. Meanwhile, Lee Mack has a makeover and theres a storm brewing on Taskmaster Island.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy works for Russell, but Mark is suspicious of Jeremys job; Mark attends a school reunion and thinks about hooking up.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: As Marks best man, Jeremy has organised a stag weekend consisting of Mark and Jeremy on a canal boat on the Shropshire Union Canal. Unfortunately, its cold, theres nothing to do, and Jeremy is instantly bored, but things soon spice up when Jeremy meets a new girl, then does something unspeakable to her pet dog. Later, Mark gets offered an exciting new job in India, possibly being his way out of marriage to Sophie.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One in the Spanish Sea
programme.desc: Carl tries to make a splash in Spain as he plans his big day with Rebecca, and he hopes she will swoon in delight at his designs.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Rex braves his first public outing while experimenting with his image at an LGBTQ+ club night. Meanwhile, a shocking turn of events at The Loft relaunch party puts all eyes on Mercedes.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Can Warren trust Jenson? Diane makes a decision as her family struggle with her prognosis. And a resident is forced to reveal the truth.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michons, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future thats about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michons owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Dave Kims parents out of town, Adam throws a party to impress Brea. Beverly lets fame go to her head after her cookbook is published.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam is torn between following Beverlys rules when it comes to off-campus lunch or going to the mall food court with his friends. After using Barrys college ID to get food at the dorm, Geoff becomes the student Barry wishes he could be.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell and Manny find out theyve got more in common than they thought, while Cameron and Gloria spend some quality time together.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Its Phils birthday and he desperately wants the years must-have gadget. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to find his inner mean machine.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The second Commitment Ceremony erupts with accusations, heartbreak and a shock exit, leaving the experiment permanently shaken.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Self-confessed princess Fiona is looking for someone chunky and funky, ex-soldier Tristan is on a mission to find his perfect woman, and Jessica loves lager and football, shes looking for a man who doesnt want to change her.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Philadelphia to visit a Mexican restaurant called Zocalo. At one point, this restaurant played Cupid to a young couple who met, fell in love and married while working here together. Greg and Mary Russell loved the place so much that they pooled their resources and bought it. However, it seems that Cupids arrow had a poisoned tip: the restaurant has become an albatross around their necks.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Lisa taunts Bart about his inability to keep a girlfriend, he convinces the family to visit New York City to find Mary Spuckler.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.desc: Violent thriller about a Vietnam veteran who turns to crime after falling on hard times. Depressed and out of work upon returning home from the war to a neighbourhood in steep decline, he decides to join a gang of criminals planning a high-risk robbery.

programme.desc: After surviving a motorcycle accident, a former boxer and longtime agnostic starts to wonder if he can use his second chance to help others, leading to the surprising realisation that hes meant to be a Catholic priest.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: A visit to East Anglia and the Midlands, where he comes across a host of specialities, including Norfolk Black turkeys, Lincolnshire chine, speciality pork pies and the champion of British cheeses, Stilton.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore a region close to Sis heart: Tyne and Wear and its great city of Newcastle, where he spent most of his life. After weeks travelling across the stunning northern countryside, the bikers are having an urban adventure.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her secrets of home cooking and celebrating.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree plays around with fuss-free food with no cooking required; she makes a ranch muffuletta sandwich; a rotisserie chicken is the secret to her Chinese chicken salad with ginger and chili dressing; she finishes with magical Mermaid Ice Cream.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes sticky toffee pudding with his mum Sally. He also rustles up the perfect steak and onion sandwich, and cooks an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie takes on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat: chicken Kiev. He also cooks spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist, and creates a deluxe shepherds pie - made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder instead of mince.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas built a fabulous library at the barn and now its time to say thanks to the team that built it. Ina prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter, and Tuscan mashed chickpeas.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its Inas idea of a perfect day - while Jeffrey runs on the beach, she whips up raspberry muffins and coffee for their breakfast. Then she heads to local restaurant, South Fork Kitchen.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Once again 12 new bakers don their aprons and head for the iconic tent in the heart of the British Countryside. The bakers first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake and their first showstopper is the classic Black Forest gateau.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Theres 11 bakers left in the tent and they face the signature challenge of creating 24 biscotti. The technical challenge is arlettes and the showstopper is an edible biscuit box of 36 biscuits of a different flavour.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once theyve reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers explore a region close to Sis heart: Tyne and Wear and its great city of Newcastle, where he spent most of his life. After weeks travelling across the stunning northern countryside, the bikers are having an urban adventure.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her secrets of home cooking and celebrating.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree plays around with fuss-free food with no cooking required; she makes a ranch muffuletta sandwich; a rotisserie chicken is the secret to her Chinese chicken salad with ginger and chili dressing; she finishes with magical Mermaid Ice Cream.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes sticky toffee pudding with his mum Sally. He also rustles up the perfect steak and onion sandwich, and cooks an Italian classic - osso buco with saffron risotto.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie takes on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat: chicken Kiev. He also cooks spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist, and creates a deluxe shepherds pie - made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder instead of mince.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas built a fabulous library at the barn and now its time to say thanks to the team that built it. Ina prepares fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter, and Tuscan mashed chickpeas.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its Inas idea of a perfect day - while Jeffrey runs on the beach, she whips up raspberry muffins and coffee for their breakfast. Then she heads to local restaurant, South Fork Kitchen.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Once again 12 new bakers don their aprons and head for the iconic tent in the heart of the British Countryside. The bakers first signature challenge is to make a Madeira cake and their first showstopper is the classic Black Forest gateau.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Theres 11 bakers left in the tent and they face the signature challenge of creating 24 biscotti. The technical challenge is arlettes and the showstopper is an edible biscuit box of 36 biscuits of a different flavour.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up a hearty chicken and vegetable pie, super-satisfying mushroom risotto, beautiful homemade bread and a no-cook chocolatey fridge cake, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a hearty fish pie, helped by his son Buddy, with a delicate fish curry made with coconut milk and spinach for the sauce.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: In the final programme of the series, Jamie Oliver cooks his versions of three comfort food classics. Theres ham, egg and chips, with a Jamaican jerk rub for the ham and served with caramelized pineapple. His ultimate chicken kebab is served with hummus and mango amba sauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Burbank, Calif., a chili-serving spot with a name dating back a century, Philadelphia, a three-story gourmet bar food joint, Toronto, Canada, a smokehouse kicking burgers and fried chicken up a notch.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Denver, a corner bar smoking pork for its biscuits and topping pizzas, in Scottsdale, Ariz., breakfast enchiladas and french toast with red wine rosemary syrup.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Philadelphia, fried PBandJs and tacos that start with the head of a pig, in Phoenix, pork sammie, and cooking everything in its wood-fired oven.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Phoenix, a regional pork dish, in Toronto, Canada, sticky Asian pork ribs and a Canadian staple.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy and the team make an erupting volcano cake for a science camp. Also theres a movie-themed cake with a projection screen inside!

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: The guys reflect on the life of Momma Mary and her legacy. While the family grieves, they also celebrate her triumphant life and vivacious personality.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas friend Dwyer Derrig is on her way back from the city and shes going to get a fabulous birthday surprise - a fridge filled with Inas Italian seafood salad and German chocolate cupcakes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its bread week in the tent and the bakers must create quick breads for the signature challenge. The technical challenge is baguettes and for the showstopper, the bakers must make edible 3D Bread sculptures.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Theres nine bakers left in the tent and the signature challenge is a French classic - creme brulee. The technical challenge tasks the bakers with making a Spanische windtorte and the showstopper calls for a stack of tiered cheesecakes.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The time has come to cross the border from Scotland to England and where better to begin this leg of the journey than James home country of Yorkshire. Here hes joined by Michelin starred chef Lisa Allen. His first stop is Whitby where hes popping into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa is paying a visit to The Black Swan.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James continues his British adventure in Lancashire with top local chef Lisa Allen in the passenger seat of his trusty red mini. Their first stop is an incredible food hall in Lisas home town of Clitheroe. Here they shop and then cook together a fantastic BBQd john dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad. Next, Lisa is in the driving seat as she takes the mini to a local buffalo farm where theyre producing some interesting cheese from buffalo milk.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen are going head to head this week for a chance to represent the North East region in the National Finals. Today its a battle for starter course, and the first chance to impress their formidable veteran chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fight for the fish course, as three of the North Easts finest chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - are out to impress. After his risky starter caused a stir yesterday Michael is planning another controversial dish in honour of the WIs support of womens rights.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the North Easts most dynamic chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - go head to head cooking their main courses. Michelin starred Tim is super keen to bag the first ten of the week, but Michael and Mini are hot on his heels.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with the dessert course. Its been a week of highs and lows, modern audacious dishes and classic masterpieces but with only two places up for grabs cooking for the judges, at the end of today who will leave the competition empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - WI president Kirsty Bowen from Sheffield - who will help decide which chef will go through to represent the North East in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Representing the North West are Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and returning contender Mark Ellis. Todays the starter course, and after failing to cook for the judges last year Marks out to redeem himself but he faces stiff competition in executive chef Eve and young gun Matt.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course, as three of the North Wests finest chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis - go head to head. Eve and Matt are hoping their classic approach to cooking fish will win the day whilst Mark is going all out with fishy take on a traditional dessert recipe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the main course for the three North West chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis. Mark remains determined and focused on his battle to get to judges chamber this year, but as the pressure to plate up perfection reaches fever pitch, will all three chefs get a dish to the pass?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North West chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow. Its the end of the road for one chef, but who will it be?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by award winning food writer Sabrina Ghayour and together theyll decide which chef will go on to represent the North West in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course for three of London and the South Easts hottest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt Gillan is determined to make it to this years banquet, but he faces fierce competition in the two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund - go head to head with their fish courses. Lee is hoping his modern plate of mackerel and passion fruit will outclass Matt and Marks more traditionally inspired salmon dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of London and the South Easts finest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt is determined to bring home a 10 with his ambitious celebration of the billy goat but has he bitten off more than he can chew?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the London and South East chefs are striving to secure one of the two spots in tomorrows Regional Final. The scores are close, so its all to play for, as today marks the end of the competition for one man.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and South East chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by Mary Gwynn, author of the official WI centenary cookbook. Together theyll decide which chef goes through to represent the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central Regions finest chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge with their starters. With big boots to fill, Angela Hartnetts protege Pip is the only newcomer in the kitchen. Will she beat the boys to claim victory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central Region chefs - newcomer Pip Lacey, 4th time competitor Richard Bainbridge and returning chef Jason Hodnett - fight it out for the fish course. Pip is hoping to outclass her rivals with a smoked haddock dish inspired by her Grandma.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with three Central Region chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett. Once again Jason is taking risks with his unusual collection of ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the Central Region chefs to impress, with the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges so with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central Region chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Looking at the legend of a terrifying giant with a seven-clawed hand who left his mark on a massive rock. This rock is sacred to the Cherokee people, but some people say the symbols arent Native.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Many mysteries remain surrounding the establishment of the Mayan city in Chichen Itzá. How was it constructed?

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Experts explore the ancient Mayan city of Tikal and ask why it was abandoned despite its dominance.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Teotihuacan was discovered by Aztecs in the 14th century. But who built such magnificent structures as these?

programme.title: Auschwitz in 33 Objects
programme.desc: A sobering look at some of the personal items and belongings left behind in Auschwitz Birkenau, a former Nazi concentration and extermination camp and the worlds largest cemetery.

programme.title: Quest for the Lost Tribes
programme.desc: Filmmaker Simcha Jacobovic searches the globe for answers and investigates the lost tribes of Israel. From 12 mentioned in the Bible only Judah and Benjamin remain after they survived the Assyrian purge of nearly three millennia ago.

programme.title: Museum Secrets
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes at some of the worlds most enchanting museums.

programme.title: Museum Secrets
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes at some of the worlds most enchanting museums.

programme.title: Museum Secrets
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes at some of the worlds most enchanting museums.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Thousands of people walk through Central Park every day unknowingly passing by a crumbling relic that connects the Big Apple to ancient Egypt.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Scott does not believe in Bigfoot, but he sure hears about it a lot. Thats because the earliest Bigfoot sightings were supposedly recorded in rocks.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher discovers what life was really like for ordinary Egyptians 3,500 years ago, during the height of the incredible civilisation of ancient Egypt. Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.title: Ancient Giants: Legends of Anakims and Nephilim
programme.desc: Anthropologists may have discovered an ancient 7-14 foot tall giant human subgroup.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Thousands of people walk through Central Park every day unknowingly passing by a crumbling relic that connects the Big Apple to ancient Egypt.

programme.title: America Unearthed
programme.desc: Scott does not believe in Bigfoot, but he sure hears about it a lot. Thats because the earliest Bigfoot sightings were supposedly recorded in rocks.

Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
